questions.statement:
<p>The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.</p>

<p>They decided to / travel in train / instead of driving / to New Delhi.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: In the following sentences, four words or phrases have been underlined. One of them is incorrect. Choose the incorrect word or phrase from the options.</span></p>

<p>Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>Mountain biking have always attracted non-conformists and strong personalities.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Directions: In the following question, some parts of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select &#39;No Error&#39;.</span></p>

<p>If that is the case, than I&rsquo;m not surprised by&nbsp;what&rsquo;s happening.&nbsp;</p>

<p>The given sentence contains an error. Select the option that correctly rectifies the error.</p>

<p>He was carefullest while stealing the crown jewels.&nbsp;</p>
options: He was carefully while stealing the crown jewels. /
He was most carefuller while stealing the crown jewels. /
He was carefuller while stealing the crown jewels. /
He was most careful while stealing the crown jewels. /

questions.statement:
<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Spot the part of the sentence that has an error in it. Mark part &#39;D&#39; as your answer if there is no error in the sentence.</span></p>

<div>He has (A) been knowing me (B) since 2019.(C) No error (D)</div>

questions.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 4.
 
Key Points
  • The primary issue in the sentence is the incorrect placement of the adverb "diligently."
  • Adverbs of manner (like "diligently") should be placed after the verb they modify or at the end of the clause.
  • The correct phrase should be "The students were studying diligently," ensuring grammatical correctness and clarity.
  • Thus, the error is located in part 4 of the sentence.
Therefore, the correct answer is 4.
 
Correct Sentence: "The students were studying diligently for their exams for weeks before you arrived."
 
Additional Information
  • 1: "No error" – This would be the correct option if there were no mistakes in the sentence, but there is an error in part 4.
  • 2: "for weeks before you arrived" – This part of the sentence is grammatically correct and does not require any changes.
  • 3: "studying for their exams" – This part of the sentence is grammatically correct and does not require any changes.
  • 4: "The students were diligently," – This part of the sentence contains an error in the placement of the adverb "diligently."
